ddls:
CREATE TABLE "monitoring_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each monitoring station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the monitoring station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographic location of the station */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the station was installed */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of sensor used at the station (e.g., Level, Quality) */,
  "sensor_model" TEXT /* Model number of the sensor */,
  "maintenance_schedule" TEXT /* Schedule for regular maintenance */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of last sensor calibration */,
  "station_status" TEXT /* Current operational status of the station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "water_quality_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data record */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the monitoring station */,
  "date_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the data collection */,
  "water_level" REAL /* Water level measurement */,
  "sigma" REAL /* Sigma value indicating water density */,
  "oxygen" REAL /* Dissolved oxygen level */,
  "ph" REAL /* pH level of the water */,
  "quality" TEXT /* Quality classification of the water sample */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Water temperature */,
  "turbidity" REAL /* Turbidity measurement */,
  "nitrate" REAL /* Nitrate level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_water_quality_data_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES monitoring_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the water quality data that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., High Water Level, Low Oxygen) */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Threshold value that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date and time the alert was generated */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  "response_status" TEXT /* Status of the response to the alert (e.g., Open, In Progress, Resolved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES water_quality_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for system login */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., technician, analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Encrypted password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role and its responsibilities */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permission levels for the role (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality_checks" (
  "check_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality check */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the water quality data being checked */,
  "check_date" TEXT /* Date the quality check was performed */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Indicates if the data is valid or not */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or notes regarding the quality check */,
  "check_type" TEXT /* Type of quality check performed (e.g., automated, manual) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("check_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_checks_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES water_quality_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location entry */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated monitoring station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the station location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the station location */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the station above sea level */,
  "watershed" TEXT /* Name of the watershed where the station is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_locations_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES monitoring_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_revisions" (
  "revision_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data revision */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the original data entry */,
  "revision_date" TEXT /* Date of the revision */,
  "previous_value" TEXT /* Previous value before the revision */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value after the revision */,
  "revision_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the revision (e.g., data validation, sensor calibration) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("revision_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_revisions_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES water_quality_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated monitoring station */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date the maintenance activity was performed */,
  "activity" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes regarding the maintenance activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_logs_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES monitoring_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instrumentation" (
  "instrument_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instrument */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated monitoring station */,
  "instrument_type" TEXT /* Type of instrument (e.g., water level sensor, water quality sensor) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Name of the instrument manufacturer */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model number of the instrument */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the instrument */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instrument_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instrumentation_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES monitoring_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each threshold */,
  "parameter" TEXT /* Name of the water quality parameter (e.g., water level, oxygen) */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Threshold value for the parameter */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert triggered by exceeding the threshold (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id")
)
